headline: Brain Drain Costs Malaysia 6,000 Skilled Graduates Annually
context: TalentCorp data indicates approximately 6,000 Malaysian graduates emigrate to work abroad each year since 2022.

cards.big: 6,000 graduates leave Malaysia annually with 72% citing better pay abroad
cards.sub: Singapore, Australia, and the UK are top 3 destinations since 2020

cards.big: Average starting salary in Malaysia is RM2,800 versus RM8,500 in Singapore 2025
cards.sub: Purchasing power gap drives migration even accounting for cost of living

cards.big: Returning Expert Programme attracted only 4,200 returnees between 2011 and 2025
cards.sub: Tax incentives have not offset the fundamental wage competitiveness gap

cards.big: World Bank estimates brain drain costs Malaysia 2% of potential GDP growth per year
cards.sub: Lost human capital accumulates as experienced diaspora builds careers abroad

cards.big: Retaining talent requires competitive wages not just patriotic appeals
Should Malaysia adopt Singapore-style salary benchmarking for public sector professionals
